reasoning: The base rate for 'Strategic developments' in Mozambique is roughly 21 per month, as shown by the reference value of 21.833 (which equals 1 + average). The question requires a >10x increase, meaning >218 'Strategic developments' must occur within a 30-day window prior to each resolution date. ACLED data usually records Strategic Developments (troop movements, arrests, looting) relatively sparingly even during active conflict. A multi-hundred monthly spike in just this category would represent an unprecedented 10-fold structural break in a country where even peak violence periods did not produce such numbers. Because it requires >218 events/month compared to ~21 normally, this would likely require widespread societal collapse and an extreme change in granular ACLED reporting. Recent updates from Cabo Ligado for May 2025 show normal, currently low-level conflict dynamics. Probabilities for all dates are kept extremely low to reflect the astronomical rarity of a true 10x surge from a non-trivial baseline. The slight increase for later dates reflects only the marginal chance of an unforeseen structural methodology change by ACLED or completely unforeseen extreme escalation long-term.
